_metadata.description: Doc Refresh Agent — fetches live AI platform documentation and writes results to source_docs/ cache files. Does not analyze or propose changes.
_metadata.instructions: Load sources list and fetch_config. Fetch stale or failed sources. Write results to cache_file paths. Never write to template files.
_metadata.usage: JSON = source list, fetch config, validation. MD = philosophy, pitfalls, usage.

companion_files.fetch_tool: update_agents/fetch_doc.py — raw HTTP fetcher with --list-links / --batch / --from-html / --check modes. Validated 2026-05-13 as a byte-identical drop-in replacement for the previous WebFetch+manual-save workflow on 11 of 11 tested sources across 5 platforms.

agent_details.description: Fetches live documentation from AI platform URLs and writes results to source_docs/ cache files for use by the doc_analysis_agent.

agent_type.description: Linear workflow: check staleness → fetch stale sources → write cache files → validate → report.

behavioral_discipline._description: Cross-cutting behavioral discipline this agent inherits. Source of truth: ../knowledge/behavioral_discipline.md (narrative) and ../knowledge/behavioral_discipline.json (structured rules).
behavioral_discipline._retrofit_note: Retrofitted post-v3.0 per Issue #8 (legacy QC handling). Original spec authored before the discipline framework existed; retrofit applied per make_agent.md → '## Behavioral Discipline (core)' → 'Retrofit mode' subsection. No system_prompt boilerplate embed (agent_type is workflow, not llm_agent — BD-QC-002 not applicable per retrofit guidance step 5).

steps.name: Check Staleness
steps.description: Read each cache_file from source_docs/. Parse the metadata header for last_fetched and fetch_status. Flag sources older than staleness_threshold_days or with fetch_status of 'failed'. Sources with fetch_method: manual are listed in the report but not fetched.
steps.output: list of stale/failed sources to fetch; list of current sources to skip

steps.name: Fetch
steps.description: For each stale or failed source, fetch the source_url. PREFERRED: `uv run update_agents/fetch_doc.py <url> --out source_docs/dropbox/<name>.md` (Tier 1 raw HTTP + bs4 + html2text — bypasses the WebFetch small-model summarization that affected the 2026-05-12 batch). FALLBACK: for sources with fetch_method='manual' (currently support.google.com Gems pages), open the URL in a browser, save as HTML to source_docs/dropbox/, then run `uv run update_agents/fetch_doc.py --from-html <path>` to convert. Retry up to max_retries on timeout / 5xx. Do not retry on 403/404.
steps.input: source URL from sources[].url; fetch_method from sources[].fetch_method
steps.output: raw fetched content + HTTP status, staged in source_docs/dropbox/

steps.name: Validate Fetch
steps.description: Before writing, verify: fetched content length > min_content_length, content is not an error page (check for 403/404 keywords in body), content contains at least one recognizable documentation element (header, list, or code block).
steps.output: validated content or fetch_status: failed with reason

steps.name: Write Cache File
steps.description: Overwrite the source_docs/ file. Construct a fresh metadata header from the source entry + today's date + fetch result. Preserve any manually added notes from the existing header by appending rather than replacing. Write header + extracted content.
steps.input: validated content + source entry + existing notes

steps.name: Report
steps.description: Output a refresh summary: sources refreshed (with old→new file size), sources skipped (current), sources failed (with reason), sources requiring manual refresh.

workflow_based.exit_point: Step 5: Report — summary of all refresh results

io_contract.description: Reads source list from this JSON, writes to source_docs/ files. Never reads or writes template files.

inputs.name: sources
inputs.format: Array of source objects from this JSON

inputs.name: target_sources
inputs.format: Optional list of specific cache_file paths to refresh. If empty, refresh all stale sources.

outputs.name: updated_cache_files
outputs.format: List of source_docs/*.md files written during this run
outputs.conditions: Only files successfully fetched and written

io_contract.side_effects: Writes to source_docs/*.md files only. Never modifies make_agent.md, make_agent.json, make_gems/make_gem.md, make_gems/make_gem.json, make_gems/make_gem_qc.md, make_gems/make_gem_qc.json, or any other repo file.

sources.label: Gemini Agentic Capabilities (function calling + Google Search)

This source now combines the 2 closest live successors: function-calling (primary) + google-search (secondary). A grounding URL attempted as a third candidate also returned 404. Multi-URL pattern matches Anthropic agent skills entry above.

sources.label: Google Gems — Use Gems in Gemini Apps
sources.section_focus: Gem creation, 4 pillars (persona, task, context, format), knowledge files, system instructions best practices
sources.notes: JS-rendered pages — not automatable via WebFetch. Manually copied from both URLs: (1) answer/15146780 (Use Gems in Gemini Apps) and (2) answer/15235603 (Tips for creating custom Gems). Covers Gem creation, 4-pillar instructions, knowledge file upload, sharing.

sources.label: Gemini API System Instructions
sources.section_focus: system instruction patterns, persona definition, task scoping, context boundaries, format guidance, best practices

sources.section_focus: AgentDefinition (description, prompt, tools, model, skills, maxTurns, permissionMode, background), parent_tool_use_id tracking, subagent invocation, stateless-by-default context, subagent resumption

sources.label: Prompt Caching

sources.label: Citations
sources.section_focus: document content block, citations.enabled=true, plain-text/PDF/custom-content variants, cited_text (no output-token cost), char/page/block indices, streaming citations_delta, incompatible with structured outputs

sources.label: Files API
sources.section_focus: Files API beta (anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14), file_id references in document/image blocks, 500MB/file 500GB/org limits, persistence until deletion, supported MIME types

sources.label: Google ADK — Multi-Agent Systems

sources.label: Google ADK — Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol
sources.section_focus: Agent2Agent protocol for cross-agent communication, 7 child pages (overview, intro, quickstart, etc.)

data_sources: 34 public AI platform documentation URLs — see sources array (9 original + 17 added 2026-05-12 wave-2 + 8 ADK A2A child pages added 2026-05-13 via fetch_doc.py)

optional_dependencies.description: No LLM required. Pure fetch + write workflow.

validation.description: How to verify the refresh agent is working correctly
pre_run_checklist: source_docs/ folder exists and is writable
All source URLs are reachable (ping test)
doc_refresh_agent.json is valid JSON with all required source fields
post_run_checklist: Each refreshed file is larger than min_content_length (500 bytes)
Each refreshed file starts with the --- metadata header
last_fetched in each header matches today's date
No template files (make_agent.md etc.) were modified
success_criteria: At least 1 source successfully refreshed per run
No source_docs file overwritten with content shorter than 20% of its previous size
refresh_summary produced with all sources accounted for

test_cases.name: Successful refresh
test_cases.input: Source with last_fetched 45 days ago, URL returns 200
test_cases.expected_output: cache_file updated, last_fetched = today, fetch_status: success, file size > 500 bytes

test_cases.name: Graceful 403 handling
test_cases.expected_output: cache_file written with fetch_status: failed, original content preserved, no retry attempted, listed in refresh_summary.failed

test_cases.name: Suspect overwrite blocked
test_cases.input: Source URL returns 200 but content is 150 bytes (< 20% of existing 9KB file)
test_cases.expected_output: Write blocked, file staged as .new for review, warning in refresh_summary

test_cases.name: Template files untouched
test_cases.input: Full refresh run on all 34 sources
test_cases.expected_output: make_agent.md, make_agent.json, make_gems/make_gem_qc.md, make_gem_qc.json are byte-identical to pre-run state

tolerance.structural: Metadata header fields must all be present

known_failures.action: Mark fetch_status: failed, do not retry, note in refresh_summary.manual_required
Mark fetch_status: failed, update source entry notes with 'URL may have moved', do not retry

known_failures.cause: JS-rendered page, error page, or redirect to login
known_failures.action: Mark fetch_status: failed, preserve original cache file, add note about JS rendering

known_failures.cause: Page returned much less content than before — possible error page or redirect
known_failures.action: Stage new content as cache_file.new for human review, preserve original, warn in summary

fallbacks.action: Abort with clear error, leave all cache files unchanged, output summary of all failures

retry_strategy.note: Only retry on transient errors (timeout, connection reset). Never retry 403/404.

error_handling.timeout_behavior: Mark as failed after timeout, continue to next source
error_handling.error_reporting: All failures included in refresh_summary with HTTP status and reason

changes: fetch_doc.py validated as drop-in replacement for WebFetch+manual-save: Phase A test ran 11 sources across 5 platforms (adk.dev, openai.github.io, developers.openai.com, ai.google.dev, docs.x.ai); 10 of 11 were byte-identical between manual save and Tier 1 raw HTTP, 1 produced 20% more content (ADK added language-tab variants upstream). Zero Tier-2 needs surfaced.
Step 2 (Fetch) updated to recommend fetch_doc.py as the preferred method, with --from-html as the fallback for manual cases. Documented in _metadata.companion_files.fetch_tool.
Refreshed source_docs/google_adk_multi_agents.md with the new +20% Tier-1 body (68KB → 82KB) to capture ADK upstream additions.

Added 2 new fetch_doc.py modes (2026-05-13): --from-html (convert browser-saved HTML to markdown without a network call) and --check (drift detection: fetch URL, compare against existing source_docs body, report % delta with NO_DRIFT / MINOR_DRIFT / MATERIAL_GROWTH / SUSPECT_REGRESSION verdicts; no file writes).
Added 8 ADK A2A child pages (google_adk_a2a_intro, google_adk_a2a_quickstart_exposing[_go|_java], google_adk_a2a_quickstart_consuming[_go|_java], google_adk_a2a_extension).
Discovered via new update_agents/fetch_doc.py --list-links mode on the adk.dev/a2a/ hub.
Fetched via fetch_doc.py Tier 1 (raw HTTP + bs4 + html2text) — bypasses the WebFetch summarization issue that plagued the 2026-05-12 refresh of openai-agents-python and ai.google.dev pages.
Companion tool: update_agents/fetch_doc.py (new in this version) — can be invoked standalone or wired into future doc_refresh_agent runs as a replacement for WebFetch.

Added 17 new sources to ground two new skills (make_orchestrator_agent and make_agent_knowledge): anthropic_subagents, anthropic_prompt_caching, anthropic_citations, anthropic_files, google_adk_multi_agents, google_adk_a2a, google_files, google_caching, openai_multi_agent, openai_handoffs, openai_agents_class, openai_running_agents, openai_tracing, openai_file_search, xai_multi_agent, xai_grok_multi_agent_model_card, xai_collections.
5 fetched clean and complete: anthropic_subagents, anthropic_prompt_caching, anthropic_citations, anthropic_files, xai_grok_multi_agent_model_card.

12 fetched as summaries (small-model WebFetch pattern, same issue noted for openai_agents_sdk and xai_overview); placeholders staged with key-fact notes; manual deep-fetch via source_docs/dropbox/ pending.
URL migrations documented: adk.dev (was google.github.io/adk-docs), developers.openai.com (was platform.openai.com — now 403/Assistants deprecated sunset 2026-08-26).
Initial version — split from update_agent.json (formerly Mode B: Refresh)
7 sources across Anthropic (3), Google (2), OpenAI (1, GitHub Pages), xAI (1)
